def: Tir. v. a. S. अभ्युत्तेजयति (?). 1. To extinguish; put out; blow out. 2. To damp; depress; discourage; dishearten. Dil bujhānā . 3. To quench (thirst); relieve, satisfy (hunger). Hai koī aisā dharmī dātā tan kī tapat bujhāvegā? Mendicant's cry. 4. To repress; cool ( ṭhanḍā karnā ); calm; appease; soothe; allay; compose; pacify; conciliate; smother (rage). 5. To temper (steel). Talwār bujhānā . 6. To thrust heated metal into water. Pānī bujhā-ke do . 7. To shuffle (the cards). Ganjfā bujhānā .
v.t. To cause to know, to make (one) understand or comprehend; to explain, inform, apprize, teach; to demonstrate; to cause to believe, to assure, persuade, convince, satisfy (syn. samjhānā).
more: v. a. S. वुध् to know. 1. To infuse into the mind; to make one understand. 2. To cause to believe; convince; satisfy; persuade.
(trans. of بجهنا bujhnā ) v. a. To extinguish, to put out, quench; to damp, depress or deject (the spirits). پياس بجهانا piyās bujhānā, To quench thirst.
(caus. of بوجهنا) v. a. To explain, to make to comprehend, to demonstrate, to push as with argument, to persuade, assure, instruct, signify, to cause to believe.

def: v.t. adj. See s.v. būjhnā. v.n. To be put out, extinguished (a fire, light, &c.); to be quenched (as thirst); to be allayed or satisfied (as hunger, or a feeling or passion); to be slaked (as lime); to be tempered (as steel); to be disinfected (as water, by having hot iron put into it); to be calmed, cooled, composed, tranquillized, &c.; to be damped (as the spirits, courage, &c.), to be dejected, downcast; to be tired or weary (of, -se); to be shuffled (as cards): — bujh-jānā = bujhnā.

ety: bujh˚ = Prk. वोज्झे(इ) or वोज्झ(इ) = S. व्यवक्षाय(ति), rt. वि+अव+क्षै
def: v.n. To be put out, extinguished (a fire, light, &c.); to be quenched (as thirst); to be allayed or satisfied (as hunger, or a feeling or passion); to be slaked (as lime); to be tempered (as steel); to be disinfected (as water, by having hot iron put into it); to be calmed, cooled, composed, tranquillized, &c.; to be damped (as the spirits, courage, &c.), to be dejected, downcast; to be tired or weary (of, -se); to be shuffled (as cards): — bujh-jānā = bujhnā.
more: v. n. S. अभ्युत्तेजयति (?). 1. To be extinguished, or put out (fire, a light, etc.). 2. To be quenched (thirst); be satisfied (hunger, passion). 3. To be dispirited, disheartened, discouraged. Lāt lāt meṅ murg bujh jātā hai . 4. To droop; hang down (the head); to be downcast. Shām se kuchh bujhā sā rahtā hai, Dil huā hai chirāg muflis kā . Mīr. At eventide my heart begins to droop and fade, As gives the poor man's lamp a dim half light, half shade. 5. To be tired, weary or sick of. Pānī tabīb de hai hameṅ, kyā bujhā huā, Hai dil hī zindagī se hamārā bujhā huā . Zauq. 6. To be well fried or browned. 1. Kachauriyoṅ kā ghān bujh gayā . 2. Kitnā sāg chaṛhāyā thā, bujh-kar itnā sā rah gayā . Wom. 7. To have a heated piece of iron, etc. thrust into water. Bujhā-huā pānī . 8. To be tempered (steel). Zahr kī bujhī-hui talwār . 9. To be shuffled (the cards).
v. n. To be extinguished, to be put out, to be quenched (thirst), to be damped or dejected (the spirits, &c.).

ety: būjh˚ = Prk. बुज्झ(इ) = S. बुध्य(ते), rt. बुध्
def: v.t. To understand, perceive, make out, comprehend; to conceive, think, consider, regard, account; to ascertain, inquire (of, se)
more: v. a. 1. To understand; comprehend ( samajhnā ). Yeh to būjhe Lāl Bujhakkaṛ, aur na būjhe koë, "Pairoṅ chākī bāṅdh-ke kahīṅ hirnā kūdā hoë ." This Lāl Bujhakkaṛ only can make out, no other can. "A mill-stone tied to his feet a bounding deer hath ran." ☞ Lāl Bujhakkaṛ was a celebrated fool among fools whose explanations of strange phenomena never failed to find acceptance. The above couplet refers to the footmarks of an elephant who had passed through during the night. 2. To ask; enquire ( pūchhnā ). Rus. Bāppū se būjh āūn̄ . W. Rus. 3. To account; rate; consider; regard; esteem.
v. a. To understand, to comprehend, to think. بوجهـ لينا būjh lenā (at cards) To settle the account of gain and loss. بوجهکي būjh-ke, Wittingly.
subs: v.n. To be understood, &c.; to appear or seem (to, -ko)
v.t. = būjhnā, q.v.; to come to an understanding (with, -se), settle accounts (with); to settle the amount of gain and loss (at cards, &c.)

more: n. m. 1. Steam ( bhāp ). 2. (Fever ( tap ). Jo ḍāl do mere pinḍe pe tum chane, bhun jāëṅ, Ziyādah is se, arī bī, bukhār kyā hogā? Wom. Rāhat. Some grain thrown on me now would roasted be, Than this, good Ma'am, could higher fever be? 3. Anger; wrath; spleen; bile; gall; resentment. 4. Sorrow; grief; anguish; affliction. bukhār chaṛh'nā, yā ānā , v. n. 1. To get fever. 2. To quake with fear; to be afraid of. Uske nām se bukhār chaṛhtā hai. bukhār nikāl'nā , v. a. To vent one's rage; pour out the phials of wrath. Khūb sā maiṅ ne nikāl apne līyā jī kā bukhār, Ho gaī āj merī uskī safāī at-gat . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. My pent up anger had today full vent, Between us now's restored complete content. bukhār nikalnā , v. n. To be discharged (grief or anger). Dil kā bukhār ro-ke na hargiz nikal sake . Sālik. No tears could e'er allay the anguish of the soul. bārī kā bukhār , n. m. Intermittent fever.

s. m. Steam, glowing heat (of the ground or such as is felt in a fever), vapour, exhalation. بخاردل مين رکهنا buḵẖār dil meṅ raḵẖnā, To entertain enmity or malice in the heart. بخارنکلنا buḵẖār nikalnā, To break out (as, an eruption on the skin).
subs: buḵẖār ćaṛhnā, v.n. To have a fever; to get into a state of feverish heat, excitement, or alarm (by reason of, -se), to be stricken with terror
To harbour, or cherish, wrath or animosity (against)
(-kā), To vent (one's) rage or spleen, to let off steam

def: adj. See s.v. A. bū. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
more: (بو Endowed with, and عجب Wonder) adj. Wonderful.

def: s.m. The septum of the nose; an ornament worn suspended from the septum of the nose, generally set with a pearl or other gem.
more: n. m. An ornament worn in the nose, mostly set with a pearl or some other gem.
s. m. An ornament worn on the nose.

def: s.m. The septum of the nose; an ornament worn suspended from the septum of the nose, generally set with a pearl or other gem.
v.t. See s.v. oulānā.
v.t. To call, send for, invite, bid, summon, cite; to challenge; — to cause to sound, to sound (as a ḥuqqā)
more: v. a. Caus. of bolnā , q. v. 1. To call; send for; invite. 1. Tīn bulāë, terah āë, de dāl meṅ pānī . Prov. Three invited, thirteen come, water the soup ad libitum . 2. Bulāë na chalāë, maiṅ to dulhan kī chachī . Prov. Nor called nor wanted, 'I am the bride's aunt.' 2. To summon; subpœnā ; require one's presence. 3. To sound; produce a sound. Huqqā bulānā .
(caus. of بولنا) v. a. To call, invite, bid.
subs: v.t. To call, &c. = bulānā; (and intens) to call off or away.

def: adj. High, lofty, tall; elevated, exalted, sublime; loud (syn. ūṅćā)
more: adj. High. See. اونچا (1—5). buland-hausalā , adj. Aspiring; ambitious; enterprising; large-minded. buland kar'nā , v. a. See اونچانا buland honā , v. n. To mount; rise up; ascend; to be elevated. buland jagah , n. f. ( Ast .) Parallax.

adj. High, elevated, lofty, exalted, sublime, tall, loud. بلندپايه buland-pāya, adj. Of high rank. بلندحوصله buland-ḥauṣila, adj. Ambitious, aspiring. بلندرتبه يامرتبه buland-rutba or -martaba, High in rank or dignity. بلندکرنا buland karnā, To lift, elevate, raise up. بلندهونا buland honā, To mount, rise up, become high, ascend. بلندنظر buland-naz̤ar, adj. Aspiring. بلندهمت buland-himmat, adj. Ambitious, high-minded. بلندهمتي buland-himmat-ī, s. f. High-mindedness, ambition.
subs: adj. Ambitious, aspiring, enterprising; large-minded, magnanimous
buland martaba, adj. Of high rank or dignity; high in rank, &c.
v.t. To lift, raise, raise up, elevate (syn. ūṅćā karnā, uṅćānā)
adj. Of lofty intention, high-minded, ambitious; — buland-himmatī, s.f. High-mindedness; magnanimity; ambition
v.n. To rise up, become high, ascend, mount, soar.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلند (buland). Compare Punjabi ਬੁਲੰਦ (buland) / بُلَند (buland), Gujarati બુલંદ (buland).

def: s.f. Height, elevation, loftiness, eminence, exaltation; pride; loudness; high land.
more: n. f. 1. Height. See اُنچائی 2. Pride. Bakht uṛ gayā, bulandī rah gaī . Prov. His grandness gone, his loftiness remains, 3. ( Ast .) Prominence ( uṅchāī ).
s. f. Height, elevation, eminence, loftiness, exaltation, loudness.

def: s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.
s.m. Calling, bidding; call, invitation, summons.
more: n. m. A call; summons; bidding; invitation.

def: s.m. = H بلاهٿ बुलाहट bulāhaṭ, s.f. = bulāwā, q.v.
s.f. (In Persia and Arabia) the nightingale; and a certain melodious bird resembling the nightingale; (in India) the fork-tailed shrike, Lanius boulboul
more: s. f. 1. A nightingale. 2. (in India) A shrike. (Lanius boulboul. Lath. ).
subs: bulbul-ćashmī, s.m. lit. 'Bulbul-eyed'; a kind of silken cloth, having a cross-bar border with an eye or dot in each of the diamond squares.
ex:
سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہَمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā hindostā̃ hamārā
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i is kī, ye gulistā̃ hamārā

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلبل (bulbul), from Arabic بُلْبُل (bulbul).

def: s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
more: n. m. S. बुद्बुद, Pālī. bubbulam . 1. A bubble. 2. A frail thing. Kyā bharosā hai ziṅdagānī kā, Ādmī bulbulā hai pānī kā .

def: s.m. pl. of balad, q.v.
(بو Endowed with, and فضول Excess) adj. Excessive talker, prodigal.
s.m. (?), Bulgaria; perfumed leather, Russia leather.
(بو Endowed with, and هوس Desire) adj. 1. Desirous. 2. Whimsical, capricious, a wavering fickle (person), a block head.
s. f. Caprice, capriciousness.
s.m. Bubble = bulbulā, q.v. s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
more: s. m. A bubble. See بلبلا bulbulā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بلا (balā), from Arabic بَلَاء (balāʔ).

def: s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), = P بلوغت bulūg̠at, s.f. (for A. بلوغة), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُلُوغ (bulūġ), from Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ).

def: s.f. (for A. بلوغة), = A بلوغ bulūg̠, s.m. (inf. n. of بلغ 'to reach'), Maturity, puberty, full age, adolescence, manhood, attaining of a marriageable age.
wety: From Arabic بُلُوغ (bulūḡ). Addition of ـت is likely from analogy with other nouns derived from Arabic, such as آخِرَت (āxirat), حِماقَت (himāqat), طَبِیعَت (tabī'at), عِبادَت ('ibādat), نَباتِیات (nabātiyāt) etc. Doublet of بُلُوغ (bulūġ).

def: s. f. Adolescence, puberty.

s.m. A large prominent eye.
v.t. To cause to call; to cause to be sent for; to send for, summon, &c. ( = bulānā)

(causal of بلانا bulānā ) v. a. To cause to call or to send for.
subs: bulwā-lenā, v.t. To send for, &c. = bulā-bhejnā, &c., q.v.s.v. bulānā.

def: n. f. Weftage; texture.

Make; frame; form; figure; shape; cut; set; build; stamp; cast; mould; fashion. 4. Show; display; ostentation; parade. 5. Artificialness; affectation; sham; counterfeit. 6. Fabrication; invention; falsehood; perjury; forgery. 7. Art; artifice; acting; gloss; false coloring. 8. Misstatement; misrepresentation; make-believe.

def: s.f. = būṅd, q.v.

def: s.f. Drop; drop of rain; — blood; semen; — a kind of spotted silk
more: pl. n. f. S. विन्द drop. 1. A drop; a drop of rain. 1. Būṅd kā chūkā, ghaṛe ḍhalkāve . Prov. The drop not poured in time a cask demands. 2. Woh būṅd vilāyat gaī . Prov. That drop has floated down to a far country. (The opportunity has flown). 2. Blood; semen. Jāne kiskī būṅd hai? Slang. 3. A kind of spotted silk. būṅd ṭapaknā , v. n. To trickle; drip; ooze. būṅd churānā , v. n. ( Slang ). To conceive; to be pregnant. Ye lugāī koṭhī kī sach chī hai, har sāl buṅd churātī hai . Slang. This woman's dark chamber is a true one, every year it retains all that is poured into it. būṅd-choṭṭī , n. f. ( Slang ). A woman prone to conceive. būṅd honā , v. n. 1. To appear as a spot (a kite). 2. To be scarcely visible; hence, to disappear.
s. f. A drop. بوندکي بوند būnd kī būnd, Rectified, twice distilled (spirit, &c.).
adj. 1. Good, well, excellent. 2. High, soaring (generally applied to kites).
subs: excellent, fine, grand, superb (the word is used, in familiar phraseology, to serve as the standard of excellence, &c., and corresponds to our phrases 'the pink of,' 'the essence of')
adv. Drop by drop, by drops; every drop of, to the last drop
v.n. To secrete semen, to conceive (a woman)
adj. Rectified, doubly-distilled (spirit, sharāb), very strong (wine)
v.n. To appear like a speck (a kite); to rise to a great height, to be scarcely visible; (hence) to disappear.

more: s. f. A kind of sweetmeat (like drops).

def: s.m. Sprig, stalk, stem; the plant of 'gram' or chick-pea (Cicer arietinum) with the green pods on it; the green pods of the chick-pea; chick-pea, gram (local); — adj. Small, diminutive, stumpy; compact and firm (as a pony, &c.; — cf. but)
more: s. m. 1. A kind of pulse when green (Cicer arietinum): see بوٿ būṭ. 2. The plant of بونٿ būnṭ. 3. adj. Little, compact and firm (epithet applied to a pony, and sometimes used substantively). بونٿ پلاو būnṭ-pulāʼo, s. m. A pulāʼo made of بونٿ.
s. (dakh.) A digit, an inch ( Harris ).

def: s.m. = būṭā, q.v. Strength, power, ability.

Būr ke laḍḍū khāë, to pachhtāë, na khāë to pachhtāë! Prov. Sweet balls of husk, eat and you'll repent, dont eat and you 'll repent. (A siren that allures to destruction).
n. f. S. भूरि दक्षणा alms in plenty. Charity or alms given in marriages, etc. Hin. būr bāṅṭnā , v. a. To give alms at a marriage, etc.

adj. Barren (land). s. f. Chaff, husk. بورکالڐو būr kā laḍḍū, A sort of sweetmeat made with the husk of grain. (The confectioners avow the imposition and cry out to passengers, "youʼll repent if you eat it, or regret not tasting it." Hence, great men are sometimes called بورکالڐو when they make a practice of encouraging their dependants to hope, but do nothing for them at last; as, dancing attendance on them and neglecting them are each likely to produce regret. The term also means, any person or thing, which is fair without and foul within, or that promises well but turns out ill; a well looking, but stupid, fellow).
subs: s.m. A sweetmeat (in the form of balls) made of the husk of grain; (fig.) one who holds out false hopes, or deludes or deceives; anything fair without and foul within, or that promises well but turns out ill; a well-looking person, or person of promising appearance, with nothing in him.
wety: Borrowed from English bore.

def: s.f. = bhūr, q.v. s.f. Hole (through which water runs out); well, or gathering pit (dug by the brink of a stream, chiefly for irrigation: syn. bhurkī).
v. n. To be drowned, to drown.
s.m. (a term of abuse), Scoundrel, rascal, villain.

adj. (f. -ī), Bad, evil, ill, wicked, vicious, evil-minded, immoral, unprincipled, vile, base, villainous, mischievous, injurious, calamitous, destructive, baneful; ugly, ill-looking; disagreeable, unpleasant; odious, hateful; — s.m. Evil, ill, harm, injury; evil person
more: n. m. pl. buroṅ , S. विरूप badly formed. 1. A wicked person; a firebrand. 1. Bure, tujh se daṛye, yā terī burāī se? Prov. Shall I fear thee, foe, or fear the evil thou canst do? 2. Bare se deo ḍarāë . Prov. Even the gods fear the wicked. 2. An enemy; antagonist; foe; rival. Ab kyā rahā ki jis se raqīboṅ kā ḍar kareṅ, Ham to buroṅ kī jān ko pahlī hi ro chuke .

adj. Opp. of achchhā good. 1. Unpleasant; disagreeable; bad ( kharāb ). 2. Ugly; ill-looking; ill-favored; repulsive ( bad-shakl ). Kyā burī sūrat hai! 3. Hateful; odious; abominable; horrible; detestable ( ḍarāonā ). 4. Distasteful; sour; bitter ( bad-mazah ). Kyā burī nāraṅgī hai, muṅh bigaṛ gayā . 5. Obnoxious; hurtful; baneful; pernicious; injurious. Burā khel hai . 6. Poisonous ( zahrīlā ); venomous; deadly; destructive. Burā kīṛā hai . 7. Mischievous; naughty; wicked; vicious. Burā ādmī hai . 8. Evil-minded; immoral; unprincipled. 9. Base; mean; vile; shameless ( be-sharm ). 10. Greedy; covetous ( lālchī ); avaricious. 11. Hard; severe; harsh; strict. 12. Unjust; unfair; inequitable. 13. Merciless ( be-rahm ); pitiless; cruel; ruthless. 14. Rugged ( aughaṭ ); rough; dangerous. 15. Unlucky; sad; deplorable; wretched ( kam-bakht ). Burī ghaṛī āī hai . 16. Peevish; sulky; sullen; iras…
adj. Bad, worse. برالگنا burā lagnā, v. n. To be unpleasant. براماننا burā mānnā, v. a. To take amiss or ill or as an affront, to be displeased or affronted. ¶ burrā, s. See جوف jauf.
adj. (dakh.) Old, old man: see بوڙها būṛhā.
subs: s.m. A sect of Brahmans who perform the obsequies of the dead (syn. aćāraj)
v.t. To depreciate, represent as bad or worthless; to detract, defame, disparage
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Middling, tolerable, passable; — bad or evil person; reproach; abusive language; — burā-bhalā kahnā, To reproach, revile, abuse
s.m. Bad state; sad, deplorable, or wretched condition; — burā ḥāl karnā (-kā), To reduce to a bad or wretched condition; to ill-treat; to ruin
s.m. Any act forbidden by law; malpractice, vice; adultery, fornication (syn. bad-fěʻlī)
(-kā), To do wrong, harm, &c. (to), to wrong, harm, injure
(-ko), To speak ill (of), to pronounce or call (one) bad, evil, wicked, &c.; to vilify, abuse
v.t. To take ill or amiss, to be displeased or angry, to be affronted, feel oneself insulted
burā zamāna, s.m. Evil hour, evil time, evil days, hard times
s.f. Bad thing; bad language, abuse; bad conduct
burī gat, s.f. = burā ḥāl, q.v.
s.f.; — bure din, s.m. = burā waqt, q.v.

def: s.m. Powder ( = buknī); saw-dust, filings (particularly of wood); coarse sugar (syn. khāṅṛ); white sugar ( = ćīnī).
more: s. m. 1. A coarse kind of sugar. 2. Sawdust. 3. Powder.
adj. (dakh.) Bad: see برا burā: s. (dakh.) A mat: see بوريا boriyā.

def: s.m. Redeemable mortgage.

n. m. lit . Bad and good. 1. Abusive language ( khoṭī kharī ). 2. Bad man; evil man. burā bhalā, burī bhalī, burā bāolā , adj. Passable; tolerable; so so ( aisā waisā ). burā bhalā kah'nā , v. a. To reproach. See āṛe hāth lenā (2 to 5). burā hāl, yā ahvāl, burī hālat, yā gat , n. 1. Deplorable or bad state; wretched condition; woful or accursed state; excessive pain; agonies; throes. 1. Nakṭā jīve bure havāl . Prov. The noseless (disgraced) lead a miserable life. 2. Ghar meṅ jiske nār chhināl, wā mānas kā burā havāl . Prov. An adultress for a wife, a curse the husband's life. 2. Straitened circumstances; poverty; misery. burā hāl karnā, patlā hāl karnā , v. a. To reduce to a bad condition; maltreat; ruin. Mār mār-kar burā hāl kar dīyā. burā chītnā, chetnā, chīn'nā, yā chāh'nā , v. n. To think ill of. Dīn o dunyā meṅ uskā hove burā, Jo kisī kā koī burā chīte . Wom. Rangin. In both worlds may they wretched be who evil do in others see. burā rāj , n. m. Mal-administration ( bad-intizāmī ). burā samā , n. m. Bad season; a season of scarcity; dearth; drought; famine ( kāl ). burā kām , n. m. 1. Any act forbidden by law, religious or secular. 2. Adultery; illicit intercourse ( bad-felī ). burā karnā , v. a. To do wrong, hurt, etc. burā likkhā kar'nā , v. a. Mah. Wom. To spoil, hurt, etc. Jān Sāhab, rāt ko phūaṛ-pane se oṛh-kar, Kyā burā likkhā kīyā tum ne hamārī shāl kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Jan Sāhab, you wore so awkwardly last night My shawl, you have indeed just spoilt it quite. burā lagnā , v. n. Opp. of achchhā lagnā . See achchhā na lagnā . burā mān'nā , v. n. 1. To take ill; take amiss. Tulsī, buro na māniye jo gaṅvār kah jāë, Sāvan kī sī nadduā buro bhalo bah jāë . Dohā. O Tulsī, mind not what the ignorant may say, As August streams both good and bad are borne away. 2. To be angry or displeased. Na burā māne to maiṅ noch lūṅ ek muṭṭhī bhar, Begmā, hai terī kiyārī meṅ harā sāg khaṛā . Wom. Inshā. I'd pluck a handful, ma'am, if you I dont offend, Of potherbs fresh which in your garden grow no end. burā waqt, yā zamānā, burī ghaṛī, bure din . 1. Unlucky star; inauspicious moment. 1. Bhagvān burī ghaṛī na lāë . Prov. Saying. May God ne'er bring on any evil days! 2. Bure waqt kā Allāh belī . Prov. In evil times the Lord our sole defence. 3. Aisī burī ghaṛī āī hai ki sone pe hāth ḍāleṅ to miṭṭī ho jāë . 2. Hard times; evil days. burā ho , intj. Perish! blast thee! See āg lage! Tumhārā burā ho! ki koṭhe pe chaṛh-ke Hazāroṅ ko tāko, hazāroṅ ko ghūro . Wom. Zafar. Heaven blast you, fellow, from the house-top standing you Your wanton eyes cast on all women in your view.

def: adj. Old, dilapidated, broken; toothless.
s.m. Filings; sawdust; powder (syn. ćūrā).
more: n. m. بريدن to cut. Filings; saw-dust; powder.

def: (from برا burā ) s. f. Mischief, badness, evil, wickedness. براٴي پراٿهناياکمرباندهنا burāʼī par uṭhnā yā kamar bāndhnā, To resolve on mischief.
more: adv. By reason of, on account of, for.

def: s.f. Badness, evil, wickedness, vice, depravity, corruptness, vileness; mischievousness, hurtfulness, destructiveness; defect, harm, mischief: dissension; slander, calumny, detraction; evil consequences or results
more: n. f. See اوگن (1, 4, 6). 1. Vileness; depravity; wickedness. Bure kī burāī meṅ, na bhale kī bhalāī meṅ . Prov. No care at all for ills of evil men, No share in aught of goodness in good men. 2. Hurtfulness; mischievousness; destructiveness; bane. 3. Calumny; detraction; slander (بدگوئي). 4. Frailty; error; weakness; infirmity. 5. Evil consequences or results. burāī bhalāī , n. f. 1. Good and evil; damage; mishap. 2. Blame; charge (الزام 1, 2).
subs: s.f. Good and evil; damage, mishap; reproach, censure, blame, accusation, &c. ( = ilzām)
burāʼī-par kamar baṅdhnā, v.n. To be bent on harm or mischief.

def: s.m. Bad man, bad character, villain; mischief-maker, slanderer; enemy.
more: n. m. 1. A bad man; bad character; mischiefmaker; firebrand. 2. A backbiter; slanderer; detractor. 3. A pilferer ( uchakkā ); a petty thief. 4. A butcher ( qasāī ) (used by herdsmen). Tohe buraīye ke kaṭwāë dūṅ . Rus. (The angry driver to the sluggish ox). 5. A policeman; sepoy. (Gamblers' idiom). Abe, buraīyā āyā, bhāgo! jī burā kar'nā , v. a. To vomit. See ulaṭnā (4).

def: s.m. Borax.
s.m. The mule on which Mohammad is said to have ascended one night from Jerusalem to heaven, and thence returned to Mecca; (met.) a swift or fleet steed.
more: Pop. n. m. برق lightning. The horse or Pegasus on which Muhammad is said to have ascended one night from Jerusalem to Heaven, and thence returned to Mecca.

adj. 1. Flashing; shining; brilliant. 2. Swift as the wind; fleet; winged. Ghoṛā kyā hai, burrāq hai . 3. Clever; expert; smart; quick; intelligent. 4. White as snow, crystal, marble, silver, or milk. Kyā burrāq kapṛe dhoë haiṅ .
n. prop. The ass on which Muhammad is said to have passed from Jerusalem to heaven.
wety: From Arabic براق (barrāq).

def: s.f. Sound of anything falling or sinking in water
more: s. A sort of sound or voice.
subs: s.f. Sound of water being poured out of a narrow-mouthed vessel; gurgling sound.

r: buraknā, per met., bukarnā, bukaṭnā, bakoṭnā
ety: per met. for bukarnā = bukaṭnā, not in use: cf. bakoṭnā
def: v.t. To sprinkle, strew ( = bhurbhurānā, q.v.). v.n. To throw or sprinkle sugar, salt, &c. (upon, -par); to long (for), &c. = bhurbhurā honā, q.v.s.v. bhurbhurā.
more: v. a. To sprinkle; strew (salt, etc.) Dāl meṅ zarā sā namak burak do .

def: s.f. Sound of a sinking body; a ball of lead for measuring the depth of water, sounding lead.
v.t. To cause to sink, &c. ( = ḍubānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to sink, to sink, dip, immerse, submerge, drown, flood; to lose, waste, exhaust, sacrifice, ruin, destroy, demolish
more: v. a. To cause to sink.

def: s.f. Food made from the egg-plant, fried brinjāl soaked in sour milk or tyre.
more: n. f. Fried brinjal soaked in tyre. Ādhī būbū, sārī rānī, jo būjhe so baṛā gyānī . Charade. Ans. Būrānī ,
s. f. A kind of food consisting of the fruit of the egg plant ( baiṅgan ) fried in sour milk.

def: s.m. = burrās, q.v. s.m. The tree Rhododendron arboreum (common on the outer ranges of the Himalaya from the Indus to Bhūṭān; also written burrāṅs, būrāṅs.
s.f. = buṛ-bhas, buṛh-bhas, q.v.
more: n. f. H. buṛ=buḍḍhā old, bhas= bhak=bhog carnal enjoyment. The affectation of youth in old age. Muṅh kālā kare kaun, lagī usko hai buṛ-bhas, Sir hiltā hai, par shauq hai missī kī dhaṛī kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Who fain would blacken her face; yet see this old thing's lickerish bent, Her head with age is shaking, still her teeth she needs must paint.

def: s.f. = buṛh-bhas, q.v. s.f. Affectation of youth in old age; state of an old dotard, doting, dotage; — s.m. = buṛhbhasiyā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = buṛ-bhasiyā, buṛh-bhasiyā.
more: adj. Affected, an old person who affects the manners of youth.

def: s.m. = buṛh-bhasiyā, q.v. s.m. One who affects in old age the manners of youth; an old dotard.
n. f. Crispiness.

ety: burā+bol, rt. of bolnā, q.v. + ā
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Foul-mouthed, abusive, scurrilous; — a foul-mouthed or scurrilous person ( = burā-bolnewālā).
more: (from برا Bad, and بولنا To speak) adj. Scurrilous.

def: s.m. Bubble ( = bulbulā, q.v.). s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
s.m. Bubble ( = bulbulā, q.v.). s.m. Bubble; any frail thing ( = budbudā).
v.t. = bhurbhurānā, and buraknā, q.q.v. v.n. To throw or sprinkle sugar, salt, &c. (upon, -par); to long (for), &c. = bhurbhurā honā, q.v.s.v. bhurbhurā.
more: v. n. 1. To murmur; mutter; grumble. 1. Dil meṅ kuchh ho to muṅh se bol uṭho, Kyoṅ gulāmoṅ se buṛbuṛāte ho? Soz. If in your mind you've aught then say it out, Why like a slave go murmuring about? 2. Baṅkkāro āj khūb, chalo maë-kade ko, Zauq, Chhoṛo kahīṅ vazīfah, bahut buṛbuṛā chuke . Zauq. Now to the tavern, Zauq, uproarious revel there, Your prayers cease, you've chattered quite enough, forbear. 2. To prate; babble; talk nonsense; rave. Buṛbuṛātī hai tū kyā, subah ko kal, rah to sahī, Haḍḍī, haḍḍī terī karnī hai mujhe chūr, dadā . Wom. Rangiṅ. Yes murmur on, my friend, but just you wait till dawn, I'll break your bones and grind them into powder, spawn.

def: s.m. See s.v. buṛ. s.f. A term of abuse applied to a woman, e.g. brimstone, strumpet, slut.

ety: v.n. — or 3rd pers. sing. pret. — of burdan, 'to carry, &c.
def: s.f. Bearing, carrying; bearing or carrying off; capture, prize, acquisition, gain, profit, haul, catch; pocketings, pickings, perquisites, bribes; stake, wager, bet; (in chess) the state in which an adversary has lost all his pieces, the king alone remaining, the game so won (it is not considered so creditable as 'check-mate')
more: n. f. بُردن to carry. See بالائي اَمد 1. Gain; profit; prize. Baṛī burd nikal gaī . 2. Perquisites; pickings; douceur; bribes. 3. Bet ( hoṛ ); wager. Āo pāṅch pāṅch rupaë kī burd sahī . Slang. 4. (In chess). The king, the only piece remaining on one side of the board. burd de'nā , v. n. To lose; miss; forfeit. Woh chāl tum chale ki dīyā burd sārā ghar, Ab bāqī maiṅ hūṅ, dāoṅ pe mujh ko lagāīye . Wom. Jān Sāhab. Your way of life hath ruin wrought, of all bereft, Now stake your wife, from all the wreck she only left. burd mȧrnā , v. a. 1. To gain a victory; win the game. 2. To have a windfall, perquisite, douceur or bribe.
s. f. A term used at chess when the king only, on one side, remains on the board; (hence, also) an opportunity of making some acquisition.
(from بردن) s. A bearing, a carrying.
subs: adj. lit. 'Bearing a burden'; forbearing, tolerant, longsuffering, patient, gentle, mild, meek; — burd-bārī, s.f. Bearing of a burden; forbearance, toleration, longsuffering, patience, meekness, gentleness
v.t. To lose, miss, forfeit; to waste, squander
v.n. To gain a victory, win the game (at chess); to make an acquisition; make a haul, have a windfall, &c.

ety: perf. part. of burdan; rt. Zend bar = S. भर्
def: part. Borne; bearing, possessing (used in comp. e.g. nām-burda, adj. Named, having the name, &c.).
= P بردباري burd-bārī, See s.v. burd.
= P بردبار burd-bār, See s.v. burd.
more: n. f. Toleration; patience; long-suffering; gentleness; meekness.
s. f. Bearing of burthen; forbearance, patience.

def: adj. See s.v. burd. s.f. Bearing, carrying; bearing or carrying off; capture, prize, acquisition, gain, profit, haul, catch; pocketings, pickings, perquisites, bribes; stake, wager, bet; (in chess) the state in which an adversary has lost all his pieces, the king alone remaining, the game so won (it is not considered so creditable as 'check-mate')
subs: adj. lit. 'Bearing a burden'; forbearing, tolerant, longsuffering, patient, gentle, mild, meek; — burd-bārī, s.f. Bearing of a burden; forbearance, toleration, longsuffering, patience, meekness, gentleness
v.t. To lose, miss, forfeit; to waste, squander
v.n. To gain a victory, win the game (at chess); to make an acquisition; make a haul, have a windfall, &c.

def: adj.& s.m. = buḍḍhā and būṛhā, q.q.v. s.m. Any wise or learned man, a sage; — the Buddha or 'enlightened,' who is regarded as the ninth incarnation of Vishṇu, and the apparent founder of Buddhism.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Old, aged; — old man ( = buḍḍhā, q.v.)
more: adj. Old; an old man. بوڙهاآرها būṛhā ārhā, Old man, eldest man.
subs: būṛhā-aṛhā (for būṛhā-bāṛhā, s.m. = būṛhā baṛā, q.v.
s.m. Reverend father; name of a saint
(or baṛā būṛhā), s.m. Respectable old man, elder, superior; experienced head of a family; patron
s.m. Affectation and blandishments of youth in old age
s.m. White top-knot, gray hairs; old age
būṛhā khappāṭ s.m. Decrepit old man; worn-out animal
v.n. To become old or aged.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀼𑀟𑁆𑀠 (vuḍḍha), from Ashokan Prakrit 𑀯𑀼𑀠 (vuḍha /⁠vuḍḍha⁠/), from Sanskrit वृद्ध (vṛddhá).

def: s.f. = buṛhāpā, q.v. s.m. Old age, senility.
Rus. n. f. A woman. Kar-kar pīt ḍigar gayo rasyā, burhal aṅg aṅg bhabūt lagāë . Song. A gay and wanton youth did take her heart and go away, the woman mourns him ashes-clad!
s.m. Demonstration, proof.
s.m. Old age, senility, senile decrepitude (syn. buṛhāpā).
s.f. = buṛhaʼǒtā, q.v.

n. m. H. būṛhā old man. Old age; the decline of life. 1. Kyā qahr hai, yāro, jise ā jāë buṛhāpā! Aur aish javānī ke taīṅ khāë buṛhāpā! Ishrat ko milā khāk meṅ, gam lāë buṛhāpā! Har kām ko, har bāt ko tarsāë buṛhāpā! Sab chīz ko hotā hai burā, hāë buṛhāpā! Āshiq ko to Allah na dikhlāë buṛhāpā! On old age. Nazīr. What vengeance falls on him to whom doth come old age! Swallowed are youthful pleasures all by dire old age! All keen delights cast in the dust, grief-bought old age! Vain thirstings after joys which come not to old age! All things, all hearts have felt thy blast, alas! old age! On lovers, God, ne'er bring that withered thing, old age! 2. Hāë buṛhāpe! Alas, old age! "Hāë buṛhāpe!"—"Javānī meṅ kyā patthar paṛte the?" "Alas, I am old now!"—"Well, but what great feat did I execute in my youth!" (The first portion is uttered in the supposed hearing of listeners. On turning round, the vain old man sees no body by. His conscience asserts itself, and he owns to himself the humiliating fact mentioned in the last portion of his speech.
s.m. Old age, senility.
wety: Related to بوڑھا (būṛhā, “old man”). Compare Punjabi بُڑھاپا (buṛā̀pā) and Saraiki بُݙھیپا (buḏhepā).

def: s.m. Old age, &c. = buṛhā-pā, &c. q.v. s.m. Old age, senility.
n. m. Old age (بُرَهاپا).
s.m. Old age, senility.

s.m. Old man ( = būṛhā, q.v.). adj. = burā; and buḍḍhā, q.q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Bad, evil, ill, wicked, vicious, evil-minded, immoral, unprincipled, vile, base, villainous, mischievous, injurious, calamitous, destructive, baneful; ugly, ill-looking; disagreeable, unpleasant; odious, hateful; — s.m. Evil, ill, harm, injury; evil person
subs: s.m. A sect of Brahmans who perform the obsequies of the dead (syn. aćāraj)
v.t. To depreciate, represent as bad or worthless; to detract, defame, disparage
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Middling, tolerable, passable; — bad or evil person; reproach; abusive language; — burā-bhalā kahnā, To reproach, revile, abuse
s.m. Bad state; sad, deplorable, or wretched condition; — burā ḥāl karnā (-kā), To reduce to a bad or wretched condition; to ill-treat; to ruin
s.m. Any act forbidden by law; malpractice, vice; adultery, fornication (syn. bad-fěʻlī)
(-kā), To do wrong, harm, &c. (to), to wrong, harm, injure
(-ko), To speak ill (of), to pronounce or call (one) bad, evil, wicked, &c.; to vilify, abuse
v.t. To take ill or amiss, to be displeased or angry, to be affronted, feel oneself insulted
burā zamāna, s.m. Evil hour, evil time, evil days, hard times
s.f. Bad thing; bad language, abuse; bad conduct
burī gat, s.f. = burā ḥāl, q.v.
s.f.; — bure din, s.m. = burā waqt, q.v.

def: s.f. Affectation of youth in old age; state of an old dotard, doting, dotage; — s.m. = buṛhbhasiyā, q.v.
adj. Affecting in old age the manners of youth.
s.m. One who affects in old age the manners of youth; an old dotard.
s.f. = buṛhiyā, q.v. s.f. Old woman; mother; a light, soft substance (vulg. called buṛhrā).

s.f. An old woman = buḍḍhī, q.v. s.f. Perception, observation, intelligence, understanding, sense, intellect, mind, wisdom, judgment, discernment; comprehension, knowledge; thought, opinion, notion, reflection
subs: s.m. Strength of intellect; mental power
adj. & s.m. Of a deteriorated intellect; bereft of reason; — deterioration or destruction of intellect
s.m. Aberration of the mind or intellect
s.m. Name of Ganeś, as the god of wisdom
buddhi-rūpī, adj. & s.m. Of the manner or nature of intelligence; — the form or embodiment of intelligence
s.m. Ocean of intelligence (an epithet and a proper name)
v.n. To enter or be impressed on the mind; to be intelligible or comprehensible
adj. Devoid of intellect; senseless, ignorant, silly, simple, foolish
(buddhi+indriya), s.m. Organ of the intellect (as distinguished from an organ of action. Six are enumerated, viz. mind, eye, ear, nose, tongue, skin).
wety: Feminine of بُوڑھا (būṛhā).

def: n. f. An old woman.
s.f. Old woman; mother; a light, soft substance (vulg. called buṛhrā).
wety: From بُوڑھا (būṛhā) + ـِیَا (-iyā).

def: adj. & s.m. Old, &c. (buḍḍhā, būṛhā, q.q.v.).
s.f. Point of a spear; spike at the end of a staff.
more: s. f. 1. A point of a spear. 2. A spike in the end of a staff.

def: part. Cut, clipped, amputated; — s.m. Field (or fields) cut by stealth by a cultivator.
more: part. pass. (from بريدن) Cut, amputated, clipped.

def: adj. Fit, or deserving, to be cut.
more: adj. Fit or deserving to be cut.

def: s.m. A diver (syn. g̠ota-ḵẖor; g̠awwāz).
s.m. Bastion, tower, turret; a sign of the zodiac; asterism, constellation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Arabic بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Classical Syriac ܒܘܪܓܐ, borrowed from Latin burgus or Ancient Greek πύργος (púrgos).

def: s.m. Cable, large track-rope (a small one being called guṇ).
more: n. m. برج It was, or became apparent, and high. 1. Bastion; tower ( gumbad ). 2. Balloon ( gubārah ). 3. A sign of the Zodiac; a constellation ( rās ).
s. m. 1. A bastion, tower, bulwark. 2. A sign of the zodiack.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Arabic بُرْج (burj), borrowed from Classical Syriac ܒܘܪܓܐ, borrowed from Latin burgus or Ancient Greek πύργος (púrgos).

def: s.f. See s.v. bur. s.f. A term of abuse applied to a woman, e.g. brimstone, strumpet, slut.
more: s. f. A term of abuse (applied to a woman).

def: s.f. Small tower, turret cut-water of the pier of a bridge.
more: n. f. A turret. Dim of burj , q. v.
s. f. A small tower, a turret.

def: s.m. A thing with which a woman veils her face, having in it two holes for the eyes (it is a long strip of cotton or other cloth, concealing the whole of the face of the woman wearing it, except the eyes, and reaching nearly to the feet)
more: (or برقعه burḳaʼaﻌ ) s. m. A dress or kind of veil with eye-holes to it, covering the whole body from head to foot. برقع پوش burḳaﻌ-posh, One who wears a burḳaﻌ.
subs: burqe-wālī, s.f. A woman who wears a burqāʻ.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بُرْقَع (burqa'), borrowed from Arabic بُرْقُع (burquʕ).

def: adj. (dakh.) Bald, bareheaded ( Harris ).
n. m. buṛkī , n. f. Bite; gripe. buṛkā mār'nā, buṛkī mārnā , v. n. To bite.
s.m. Clutch, gripe; snap, bite ( = buṛkī)
s.m. A small cake of cow-dung with a hole in the middle, placed on the mouth of a vessel, &c.
more: E. n. m. Hin. A small cake of cow-dung with a hole in the middle, thrown over the Holikā . q. v.

def: v.n. = buraknā, q.v. v.n. To throw or sprinkle sugar, salt, &c. (upon, -par); to long (for), &c. = bhurbhurā honā, q.v.s.v. bhurbhurā.
s.f. = buṛkā, q.v.
subs: burqe-wālī, s.f. A woman who wears a burqāʻ.

def: s.f. Dive, dip, plunge ( = ḍubkī, q.v.)

r: burkī, per met., bukrī, bukṭī, bukṭā
ety: per met. for bukrī = bukṭī, dim. of bukṭā, q.v.
def: s.f. A pinch (as of salt, &c.); a pinch of dust used as a charm; (hence) charm, enchantment
more: n. f. A pinchful of dust used as a charm. Hence, charm; enchantment; sorcery; magic. burkī mārnā, burkī ḍāl'nā , v. a. 1. To charm; bewitch; practice sorcery. 2. To enchant; fascinate; captivate. Maiṅ āge zanākhī ke kuchh bol nahīṅ saktī, Kyā jānye kyā us ne mārī hai mujhe burkī . Wom. Rangīṅ.
subs: (-par), burkī mārnā, v.t. To charm, enchant, bewitch.

def: s.m. A small kind of wasp.

(black-faced), n. f. Wom. H. burā bad, muṅh face. 1. A forbidding countenance; an ill-looking or ugly person; a wry face; an eye-sore. 2. A cat ( billī ). bur-mūhī , adj. Foul; dirty; nasty. Phr. Ugly as sin; ugly as a toad; ugly as a dead monkey.
v.n. = būṛnā, q.v.

ety: būṛ˚ = Prk. वुड्ड(इ) = S. व्रुड(ति), rt. वुड्
def: v.n. To dip, be dipped, be immersed; to dive; to sink, be drowned ( = ḍūbnā)
more: v. n. 1. To dive. 2. To be immerged, to drown, to dip.
subs: v.n. To die by drowning, be drowned, to drown.

def: s.m. = būṛhā-pan; buṛhāpā, qq.v. s.m. Old age, senility.

s.m. Interior cavity, hollow; anything that is hollow within (as a drum, dry pumpkin, &c.); the seed of the kusūm plant; pimple, blotch, eruption (on the skin).
more: E. n. m. 1. A pimple; blotch; eruption on the face ( muhāsa ). 2. The seed of the kusūm plant ( kaṛh ).

def: adj. Sharp, cutting.
s.m. = burās, būrāṅs, q.q.v.
s.m. The tree Rhododendron arboreum (common on the outer ranges of the Himalaya from the Indus to Bhūṭān; also written burrāṅs, būrāṅs.
more: n. m. A hill tree bearing a red flower.
(from برا burā ) s. f. (dakh.) 1. Badness, evil. 2. Vexation, regret.

def: s.f. Sowing by dropping seed into the furrow from the hand (instead of sowing broadcast or by drill).
more: n. f. lit . A stream of grain or seed. Sowing seed by dropping it from the hand into the furrow, instead of sowing broadcast or by drill.

def: s.f. = burrī, q.v. s.f. Sowing by dropping seed into the furrow from the hand (instead of sowing broadcast or by drill).

ety: abst. s. from burrīdan; see burrān
def: s.f. Cutting; sharpness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برش (buriš).

def: s.f. Earthen pot or bowl for fire; crucible (of a goldsmith, &c.; syn. gursī).
more: Bundal kand. n. f. Any vessel in which fire is kept.
wety: From بَرَس (baras, “year”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. Coldness, coolness, chilliness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian برودت (burūdat), from Arabic بُرُودَة (burūda).

def: s.m. Bastions, towers; signs of the zodiac.
more: s. plur. (of برج) Bastions, towers, signs of the zodiack.

def: s.m. Cane-weavers; basket-maker; mat-maker ( = P. boriyā-bāf).
s.m. = bhus, q.v. s.m. Husk (of com), straw; chaff, bran
subs: v.n. To make the chaff fly; to beat one to pieces, make mincemeat of; — to lament, mourn, bewail (as the Shīʻas do in Moḥarram)
v.n. To cause to be filled or stuffed with straw; to flay (a person), and fill the skin with straw and burn it (an oriental mode of punishment)
(-meṅ), To fill or stuff with straw, chaff, or or bran
s.f. lit. 'Assignment on chaff'; an assignment from which nothing can be obtained, a valueless assignment.

def: v.n. To smell fetid, to stink (as rotten meat or food); to become rotten; to become sticky.
more: v. n. To become sticky. See بُسنااُبس؟نا
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: s.m. Coral, and its root.
s.m. Rose-hower; place abounding with sweet-smelling flowers or fruits; rose-garden.
adj. Rude, impudent, audacious, insolent, presumptuous ( = gustāḵẖ, q.v.).
s.f. Rudeness, &c. ( = gustāḵẖī, q.v.). s.f. Presumption, arrogance, insolence, audacity, assurance, sauciness, rudeness; contempt (of court); cruelty
subs: To presume, to be arrogant, or insolent, or rude, &c.

def: s.m. Flower-garden ( = būstān or bo-stan q.v.).
more: s. m. A flower-garden. See بوستان bo-stān.

r: būstān, bostān, bo, stān, lit., Saʻdī
def: (bo + stān), s.m. lit. 'Place of fragrance'; flower-garden; pleasure garden; name of a celebrated work by the Persian poet and moralist Saʻdī.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بوسْتَان (bōstān). By surface analysis, بو (bo, “fragrance”) + ـسْتان (-stān, “place of”).

def: adj. Relating to a flower-garden; of the flower-garden.
s.m. Idol, image, statute; a beloved object, mistress ( = māshūq); blockhead, dolt; — adj. Stock-still, speechless, dumb; stupefied, senseless
more: n. m. S. पुत्तल 1. An image; idol; statue ( mūrat ); effigy. 2. A beloved object; a mistress ( māshūq ). 3. A blockhead; dolt; fool; idiot; sot. 4. An inclined plane along which dice or cowries are rolled. (Gamblers' idiom). 5. ( buttā ). A blow; a box on the ear.
adj. 1. Silent; speechless; dumb ( gum sum ). 2. Stupefied; senseless ( be-hosh ); mad. but bannā,—honā , v. n. To become stiff and dumb as a statue. Nashe meṅ but banā huā hai .
s. m. 1. A hazard table. 2. A blow with the fist.

s. m. An idol, image, statue; a beloved object, a mistress. (Plur. بتان). بت پرست but-parast, An idolater. بت برستي but-parast-ī, s. f. Idolatry. بت تراش but-tarāsh, A sculptor, a carver of idols. بت تراشي but-tarāsh-ī, s. f. The art of a sculptor, sculpture, statuary. بتخانه but-ḵẖāna, s. m. An idol-temple.
subs: To turn into a statute, become rigid and speechless as a statue
s.m. A worshipper of images, an idolater; — but-parastī, s.f. Image-worship, idolatry
s.m. A carver of images or idols; sculptor
s.f. Sculpture, statuary; the art of a sculptor
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. Hazard-table, an inclined plane along which dice or cowries are rolled (in gambling); — blow with the fist; box on the ear; cuff ( = buttā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

more: s. m. A kind of pulse, chick-pea. (Cicer arietinum). ¶ boṭ (dakh.) s. See انگل.
wety: Borrowed from English boot.

def: Strength, power, ability.
s.m. Flower, sprig (particularly worked on cloth, or painted on paper); bush, shrub
more: s. m. A flower (particularly, worked on cloth or painted on paper, &c.), a bush, a shrub. بوٿا قد būṭā-ḳadd, is applied to describe the small stature and exquisite proportion of a beloved person. بوٿي دار būṭe-dār, Flowered (cloth).
subs: būṭā-sā qad, adj. Of small stature; tiny; of lovely and delicate proportion
(-par), To work flowers (on cloth), to embroider

def: s.m. Crucible, melting-pot; shrub, spreading bush (cf. būṭā).
more: n. m. 1. The young of a camel. 2. ( Met .) A fat person; an ill-looking person.
s. m. A crucible, a melting pot.

def: Strength, power, ability.
more: Wom. n. m. S. वित्तम् power. Strength; power; ability. 1. Bārah baras ke rānī ke dulhā, Rām, more būte barjo na jāë! Marriage song. The Rām's husband now grown twelve years old, O Rām, he will not be by me controlled. 2. Kamar na būtā, sāṅjhe sūtā! Prov. No strength in his loins and early to bed!

n. m. būṭī, būṅṭī , n. f. 1. A flower, particularly worked on cloth, or painted on paper. 2. A bush; a shrub. būṭe-dār , adj. Flowered (cloth); chequered. būṭā sā qad , adj. Of small stature; tiny. Woh dum phundnā sī aur būṭā sā qad, uskā ahā hā hā! Nazīr. būṭā kāṛh'nā , v. a. To embroider.

def: s.f. A little idol.
v.t. To extinguish, put out (fire, light, &c.; syn. bujhānā).
more: E. v. a. To put out the light (بجهانا). Dhadhāī se butāī . Mag. Prov. Blazed, and blown out!
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. Force, violence.
s.m.? A term applied to an extra cess upon a cultivator.

ety: contrac. of buyūt-āt, pl. of bait
def: s.m. Account of household expense; marketing charges (cf. buyūt).
more: (properly, بيوتات) s. m. Account of household expense.

def: adj. Relating to household expense.
s.f. Explaining, instructing ( = bujhāʼī).
